pageTargets.desktopGoal: Dense operational overview with KPI strip, priority queue, quick modules, and recent orders in one viewport.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Compact metric strip and actionable cards under a floating list header.

pageTargets.desktopGoal: Queue, filters, dense rows, and optional quick preview panel for desktop only.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Reference only; do not redesign mobile order information, detail, or work-order pages.

pageTargets.desktopGoal: One-page CRM workspace with search/list, profile/devices, and activity/orders/follow-ups.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Dense customer cards and detail hierarchy borrowed from mobile order detail.

pageTargets.desktopGoal: KPI, scan/search, supplier/import status, dense SKU table, and detail/finance panel.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Search/scan entry, stock cards, detail cards for supplier, finance, attachments, and history.

pageTargets.desktopGoal: Guided quote, model/condition choices, margin checks, attachments, and records on one workspace.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Compact quote workflow with card-based choices and mobile-detail-like hierarchy.

pageTargets.desktopGoal: Thread-first workspace with conversation list, message thread, order/customer context, and templates.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Thread list first with compact template/action rows.

pageTargets.desktopGoal: Store, roles, workflow transitions, templates, and audit readiness in dense section cards.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Section card list with compact switch and workflow rows.

pageTargets.desktopGoal: Request queue, approval detail, role/tenant summary, and policy notes in one decision surface.
pageTargets.mobileGoal: Dense request cards with applicant, tenant, reason, status, and actions.

desktopZones: KPI strip: today orders, waiting quote, unpaid balance, ready pickup
Priority queue: blocked, overdue, ready, and needs-customer-response cards
Recent orders: dense list with customer, device, stage, payment, and next action
Quick modules: buyback, inventory scan, message templates, and settings shortcuts
Risk rail: partial data warnings, low-stock warnings, and delayed pickups
mobileZones: Floating header with store/day context and primary add action
Horizontal metric strip that fits 3-4 cards without wrapping
Priority business cards with status, amount, and next action
Recent activity cards using the mobile order-detail information hierarchy
Bottom action row for add order, scan, and WhatsApp follow-up

densityTargets.desktop: First viewport shows 4 KPIs, 6 priority/recent records, and quick modules without scrolling.
densityTargets.mobile: First viewport shows header, metrics, and at least 3 actionable cards.

motionFlows: KPI click filters the queue with motion/fast chip feedback.
Priority card opens context rail or sheet with motion/standard.
Partial-data warning resolves with motion/instant badge and toast.
acceptance: No hero or decorative chart dominates the first viewport.
Desktop warnings and next actions are visible without page navigation.
Mobile card hierarchy matches the protected order-detail standard.

desktopZones: Queue toolbar: search, status stepper, quick filters, date/owner filters, export/print
Dense table: customer, device, issue, stage, payment, owner, next action, updated time
Batch bar: visible only when rows are selected
Desktop preview rail: order context, status, finance, history, and next action
Protection note: mobile order information/detail/work-order pages are locked
mobileZones: Protected reference note only
Locked hierarchy map: floating header, info cards, history, bottom actions
No replacement frame for mobile order list, mobile detail, or mobile work-order management

densityTargets.desktop: First viewport shows toolbar, 12-16 dense rows, and the optional preview rail.
densityTargets.mobile: Reference-only frame confirms protected scope; no redesign target.

motionFlows: Row click opens preview rail with motion/standard translateX.
Filter panel expands inside toolbar with motion/fast.
Batch selection reveals stable batch bar without changing table width.
acceptance: No Figma frame implies replacement of protected mobile order pages.
Desktop queue keeps table width stable during filter and preview interactions.
Preview rail is desktop-only and marked as non-mobile.

desktopZones: Left rail: customer search, filters, tags, and dense customer list
Profile workspace: identity, phones, devices, tags, preferences, and edit actions
Activity rail: orders, follow-ups, messages, timeline, and next action
KPI/header addon: active customers, unpaid balance, follow-ups due, devices
State panels: load error, empty search, refresh warning, and pagination
mobileZones: Floating list header with search and create customer action
Customer business cards with phone, device count, tags, and next action
Detail sheet hierarchy: recent history, profile, devices, orders, follow-ups
Compact contact/action row for WhatsApp, call, message, and edit
Status and tag chips reuse the shared badge component

densityTargets.desktop: One-page CRM shows list, profile, and activity together at 1440x1024.
densityTargets.mobile: First viewport shows header/search plus at least 4 customer cards or detail sections.

motionFlows: Selecting a customer updates profile/activity with motion/standard.
Tag and follow-up edits use motion/instant pending state plus toast.
Mobile card opens detail sheet using protected order-detail hierarchy.
acceptance: Customer list/detail keeps high information density without nested cards.
Mobile detail order follows history -> profile -> devices -> orders -> actions.
Error and empty panels use Business Card status-notice styling.

desktopZones: KPI strip: total stock, low stock, buyback source, margin risk
Scan/search toolbar: barcode, supplier, import, stock state, condition filters
Dense SKU table: product, supplier, quantity, cost, price, margin, status
Detail/finance rail: checks, attachments, ledger, source, and history
Import preview state: mapping summary, apply result, and failure recovery
mobileZones: Search/scan entry under floating header
Horizontal chips for stock, low, supplier, buyback, import
Stock cards with product, quantity, status, cost/price, and next action
Detail cards for identity, supplier, risk, finance, attachments, and history
Bottom actions for scan, adjust stock, import, and handoff

densityTargets.desktop: First viewport shows KPI strip, toolbar, 10-14 SKU rows, and detail rail.
densityTargets.mobile: First viewport shows scan/search, chips, and at least 3 stock cards.

motionFlows: Scan result opens detail rail with motion/standard.
Import preview applies with motion/fast status notice.
Stock adjustment keeps row dimensions stable and shows inline pending.
acceptance: Financial and supplier data are visible together for decision making.
Mobile keeps scan and stock actions reachable without long scrolling.
Import/error/empty states use shared Business Card and Info Tile targets.

desktopZones: Guided quote wizard: series, model, storage, battery, condition, documents, signature
Quote/margin panel: estimated value, deductions, target price, margin, and risk
Attachment/proof area: camera, photos, PDF/document proof, and reset action
Record list: active quotes, inventory handoff, customer/device context
State panels: empty state, validation errors, selected estimate, and handoff result
mobileZones: Floating header with quote state and create/continue action
Stepper chips for quote stages
Business-card choice rows for every selection group
Quote detail cards for customer, device, finance, proof, and history
Bottom actions for save quote, WhatsApp, approve, and inventory handoff

densityTargets.desktop: First viewport shows wizard, quote/margin, and record context together.
densityTargets.mobile: First viewport shows current step, 4-6 selectable cards, and quote summary.

motionFlows: Choice card selection updates quote with motion/instant.
Attachment capture confirms with motion/fast notice.
Inventory handoff opens confirmation with motion/standard.
acceptance: No marketing-style hero; quote creation is the primary first screen.
Every choice row maps to the shared Business Card target.
Finance emphasis is visible before the final handoff action.

desktopZones: Thread list: customer, channel, unread/failed state, linked order, last message
Conversation panel: message history, composer, send preview, and attachments
Template/variable rail: template health, enable toggle, variable insertion, preview
Order/customer context: status, next action, amount, and contact actions
State panels: template load error, no-template empty, no-match group, send failure
mobileZones: Thread-first floating header with search and compose action
Compact thread cards with customer/order/status context
Conversation detail sheet with template insertion rows
Template and variable rows use compact action-card layouts
Bottom actions for send, template, WhatsApp, and order link

densityTargets.desktop: First viewport shows thread list, active conversation, templates, and context.
densityTargets.mobile: First viewport shows header/search and at least 5 thread rows or template action rows.

motionFlows: Thread select updates conversation with motion/standard.
Template insert gives motion/instant inline feedback.
Save settings disables action and shows motion/fast toast.
acceptance: Template health and send preview are visible without leaving the message page.
Variable rows and toggles use shared Business Card action-row styling.
Mobile thread flow stays compact and does not hide linked order context.

desktopZones: Store profile and business identity
Members and roles with invite/permission state
Workflow transitions with target rows and checkbox/action controls
Templates and notification configuration
Audit readiness, load error, and retry/recovery states
mobileZones: Section-card list under floating header
Role/status rows as compact Business Card controls
Workflow target rows with selected/disabled/pending states
Template and audit rows with compact switches/actions
Error and recovery panel with retry action

densityTargets.desktop: First viewport shows store, members/roles, workflow, templates, and audit summary.
densityTargets.mobile: First viewport shows header plus 4-5 section cards or workflow rows.

motionFlows: Workflow target toggles use motion/fast inline pending.
Save settings uses motion/instant disable and motion/fast toast.
Section expand/collapse uses motion/standard while preserving row height.
acceptance: Settings reads as an operations console, not a decorative landing page.
Permission/disabled states are visible and keyboard-focusable.
Workflow rows share the same action-row component language.

desktopZones: Access request queue with applicant, tenant, reason, submitted time, and status
Decision context rail with role, policy, risk notes, and tenant summary
Approve/reject action area with pending, error, and success feedback
Governance notes and audit-ready history
State panels for empty queue, load error, selected request, and mocked QA state
mobileZones: Dense request cards with applicant, tenant, role, reason, and status
Decision sheet using order-detail-like hierarchy
Approve/reject actions remain visible at the bottom
Policy/risk notes rendered as compact info cards
Empty/load error cards use shared Business Card styling

densityTargets.desktop: First viewport shows queue, selected decision context, and policy summary.
densityTargets.mobile: First viewport shows header and at least 4 request cards or full decision sheet.

motionFlows: Request select opens decision context with motion/standard.
Approve/reject disables actions and shows motion/fast toast.
Resolved request exits queue with motion/fast opacity only in reduced motion.
acceptance: Decision context is visible before approve/reject actions.
No production data or secrets are exposed in design/test screenshots.
Empty and error states align with the shared status-notice component target.

mobile.target: floating header plus compact business cards

visualPolishRules: Use neutral card surfaces, thin borders, and compact shadows; color is reserved for status, risk, primary action, and current workflow state.
Desktop targets must prioritize scan density: KPI strip, toolbar, main work area, and context panel should fit in the first 1024px viewport when feasible.
Mobile targets must borrow the protected order-detail hierarchy: floating header, recent history, compact info cards, finance emphasis, attachment/history access, and bottom actions.
Avoid decorative gradients, oversized hero sections, nested cards, and large marketing-style empty space.

principles: Motion communicates state, hierarchy, and feedback; it is not decorative.
High-frequency list and form operations must feel instant and must not wait for long transitions.
Animate opacity and transform first; avoid size animations that reflow dense tables or card lists.
Every motion pattern must have a reduced-motion equivalent.
motionSystem.reducedMotion: Disable translate/scale movement, keep instant opacity and state changes, and preserve all visible status text.

prototypeFlows.name: Desktop list selection
prototypeFlows.result: Right context panel opens without leaving the list; selected row and toolbar batch state update.

prototypeFlows.name: Desktop filter refinement
prototypeFlows.result: Filter panel expands inside the toolbar area; table keeps layout width stable.

prototypeFlows.name: Mobile card detail
prototypeFlows.result: Detail sheet uses floating-header hierarchy and keeps bottom actions visible.

prototypeFlows.name: Mutation feedback
prototypeFlows.trigger: Save, status change, send, approve, or import action
prototypeFlows.transition: motion/instant button loading, then motion/fast toast
prototypeFlows.result: Button disables during pending; inline error or success toast appears without shifting layout.

prototypeFlows.name: Skeleton to content
prototypeFlows.result: Skeleton occupies the final layout dimensions so dense pages do not jump.
